lede: A look at January 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jarell Quansah and Marc Gasol, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: American Eagle (airline brand)
events.desc: American Eagle Flight 5342 collided mid-air with a Sikorsky UH-60 Black Hawk operated by the United States Army and crashed into the Potomac River, killing all 67 people on board both aircraft.

events.title: Quebec City mosque shooting
events.desc: A gunman opens fire at the Islamic Cultural Centre of Quebec City, killing six people and wounding 19 others in a spree shooting.

events.title: SCAT Airlines Flight 760
events.desc: SCAT Airlines Flight 760 crashes near the Kazakh city of Almaty, killing 21 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The first day of the Battle of Rennell Island, USS Chicago (CA-29) is torpedoed and heavily damaged by Japanese bombers.

events.title: Mexican Revolution
events.desc: Mexican Revolution: Mexicali is captured by the Mexican Liberal Party, igniting the Magonista rebellion of 1911.

events.title: Charles Curtis
events.desc: Charles Curtis of Kansas becomes the first Native American U.S. Senator.

events.title: Liliʻuokalani
events.desc: Liliʻuokalani is proclaimed the last monarch and only queen regnant of the Kingdom of Hawaii.

events.title: Queen Victoria
events.desc: Queen Victoria issues a Warrant under the Royal sign-manual that establishes the Victoria Cross to recognise acts of valour by British military personnel during the Crimean War.

events.title: War of the Sixth Coalition
events.desc: War of the Sixth Coalition: France engages Russia and Prussia in the Battle of Brienne.

events.title: Pope Sergius III
events.desc: Sergius III is elected pope, after coming out of retirement to take over the papacy from the deposed antipope Christopher.

births.name: Ernst Lubitsch
births.line: German American film director, producer, writer, and actor (died 1947)

deaths.text: Salwan Momika, Iraqi refugee and anti-Islam activist
Jack A. W. Bennett, New Zealander literary scholar
Elihu Vedder, American symbolist painter, book illustrator and poet

observances.text: Christian feast day: Sulpitius I of Bourges
Christian feast day: Blessed Villana de' Botti
Earliest day on which Fat Thursday can fall, while March 4 is the latest; celebrated on Thursday before Ash Wednesday. (Christianity)
